# transmit(_:reply:)

**Instance Method**

Transmits data in Application Protocol Data Unit (APDU) format to the Smart Card.

## Declaration

```swift
func transmit(_ request: Data, reply: @escaping @Sendable (Data?, (any Error)?) -> Void)
```

```swift
func transmit(_ request: Data) async throws -> Data
```

## Parameters

- **request**: The APDU request data.
- **reply**: 



The `NSError` object is created in the [doc://com.apple.cryptotokenkit/documentation/CryptoTokenKit/TKErrorDomain] domain with a code in the [doc://com.apple.cryptotokenkit/documentation/CryptoTokenKit/TKError/Code] enumeration.

## Discussion

You should only call this method after a session to the Smart Card has been established using the [doc://com.apple.cryptotokenkit/documentation/CryptoTokenKit/TKSmartCard/beginSession(reply:)] method, and before the session is terminated using the [doc://com.apple.cryptotokenkit/documentation/CryptoTokenKit/TKSmartCard/endSession()] method.

## Communicating with the Smart Card

- **beginSession(reply:)**: Begins a session with the Smart Card.
- **endSession()**: Completes any pending transmissions and ends the session to the Smart Card.
